overview.introduction: Docker Compose is a declarative tool for defining and running multi-container Docker applications. By using a single YAML configuration file, you can manage the build, network connection, and run properties of multiple services at once.
overview.why_it_matters: Production web systems are almost never single-process containers. They require frontends, application API backends, cache endpoints, and persistent databases. Running separate 'docker run' commands for each is highly error-prone. Compose automates this stack entirely.
overview.industry_usage: Used by engineering teams to deploy replica environments instantly, run local integration testing suites, and manage standard microservices dependencies with unified parameters.
overview.what_you_will_build: A multi-tier service layout incorporating Nginx and Redis services defined inside a docker-compose.yml file, managed entirely using Compose CLI actions.
learning_outcomes: Verify syntax configuration layouts using config validations.
Manage group container lifecycle state transitions.
Audit logs and run exec commands within compose contexts.
Teardown compose network, volume, and service resources.

key_concepts.title: Compose YAML Syntax
key_concepts.definition: Docker Compose uses YAML files (docker-compose.yml) to define services, networks, and volumes. Services represent running container templates.
key_concepts.explanation: A service definition specifies the container image, host port forwards, volume directories, networks to join, and environment keys.

commands.name: docker compose config
commands.purpose: Verify syntax configurations of the YAML file.
commands.expected_output:
services:
  db:
    image: redis
  web:
    image: nginx
commands.output_explanation: Validates the structure and resolves path variable keys, printing parsed content.

key_concepts.title: Compose Stack Lifecycles
key_concepts.definition: Compose manages groups of containers together. The 'up' command starts all services; 'down' stops and removes all containers, networks, and volumes.
key_concepts.explanation: This simplifies dev pipelines. Instead of writing five separate docker network, volume, and run commands, one command spins up everything.

commands.name: docker compose up
commands.purpose: Build, create, start, and attach services.
commands.expected_output:
Creating network "student_default"
Creating student_db_1 ... done
Creating student_web_1 ... done
commands.output_explanation: Starts Nginx and Redis in detached mode (-d), allocating a default bridge network.

commands.name: docker compose down
commands.purpose: Stop and remove containers, networks, volumes, and images.
commands.expected_output:
Stopping student_web_1 ... done
Stopping student_db_1 ... done
Removing network student_default ... done
commands.output_explanation: Shuts down running containers and tears down network configurations.

lessons.title: 1. Write docker-compose.yml
lessons.definition: Creating the stack description blueprint file using YAML configurations.
lessons.explanation: Create the file listing two services: web (using nginx) and db (using redis).
lessons.instruction: Create docker-compose.yml in the home directory defining web (nginx) and db (redis) services.
lessons.example:
echo -e "version: '3'
services:
  web:
    image: nginx
  db:
    image: redis" > docker-compose.yml
lessons.hint:
Type: echo -e "version: '3'
services:
  web:
    image: nginx
  db:
    image: redis" > docker-compose.yml
lessons.solution:
echo -e "version: '3'
services:
  web:
    image: nginx
  db:
    image: redis" > docker-compose.yml

lessons.title: 2. Verify Compose Configurations
lessons.definition: Validating compose config file syntax format properties.
lessons.instruction: Validate the docker-compose.yml layout using docker compose config.

lessons.title: 3. Launch Compose Services
lessons.definition: Starting all multi-container services in detached background mode.
lessons.instruction: Launch compose services in background using docker compose up -d.

lessons.title: 4. List Stack Containers
lessons.definition: Checking status metrics specifically for compose managed containers.
lessons.instruction: Verify running compose containers using docker compose ps.

lessons.title: 5. List Compose Service Images
lessons.definition: Viewing images associated with the active compose services stack.
lessons.instruction: View details of compose images using docker compose images.

lessons.title: 6. Stream Service Log Outputs
lessons.definition: Auditing log records from all container services running inside the compose stack.
lessons.instruction: Check log streams from compose services using docker compose logs.

lessons.title: 7. Execute Command inside Database Service
lessons.definition: Running diagnostics processes within specific active services.
lessons.instruction: Ping the Redis database service using docker compose exec db redis-cli ping.
lessons.example: docker compose exec db redis-cli ping
lessons.hint: Type: docker compose exec db redis-cli ping
lessons.solution: docker compose exec db redis-cli ping

lessons.title: 8. Teardown Services and Clean Networks
lessons.definition: Shutting down container runtimes and clearing associated networking channels.
lessons.instruction: Halt and delete compose structures using docker compose down.

exercises.title: Map Service Ports
exercises.problem: Modify the web service in docker-compose.yml to forward host port 8080 to container port 80. Launch the stack and test.
exercises.hint: In compose yaml, use ports: - '8080:80' under the web service block.
exercises.expected_result: docker compose config shows the port mappings correctly.
exercises.objective: Understand port forwards inside compose configurations.

exercises.title: Use Restart Policies
exercises.problem: Set restart: always for services in compose configurations to make them resilient against service daemon restarts.
exercises.hint: Add restart: always key level under each service tag block.
exercises.expected_result: restart directives visible in config outputs.

quiz.question: What is the default filename used by Docker Compose?
quiz.explanation: Compose looks for docker-compose.yml (or docker-compose.yaml) in the current directory by default.

quiz.question: Which command halts compose containers and removes networks?
quiz.explanation: 'docker compose down' stops container runtimes, deletes containers, and cleans the bridge networks.

quiz.question: How do you start compose services in detached background state?
quiz.explanation: The -d (or --detach) option runs all service processes in the background.

quiz.question: What command lists statuses of containers managed by the active compose file?
quiz.explanation: 'docker compose ps' lists statuses, ports, and names of stack services.

quiz.question: How do you view log stdout streams from a single compose service (e.g. 'web')?
quiz.explanation: Passing service name to 'docker compose logs [service]' filters logs stdout stream for that service context.

quiz.question: What does 'docker compose config' perform?
options: Verifies and displays parsed yaml configuration contents.
quiz.answer: Verifies and displays parsed yaml configuration contents.
quiz.explanation: 'docker compose config' parses syntax formatting errors, resolves paths, and prints final configurations.

quiz.question: Which directive specifies container dependency startup orders (e.g. web waits for db)?
quiz.explanation: The 'depends_on' key enforces service start orders, letting you start databases before API web apps.

quiz.question: How do you run a shell command inside an active compose container (e.g., 'db')?
quiz.explanation: 'docker compose exec [service] [cmd]' runs a command inside the active, running container instance.

quiz.question: How does Docker Compose handle container names by default?
options: Prefixes them with the directory name and appends service name and number.
quiz.answer: Prefixes them with the directory name and appends service name and number.
quiz.explanation: To prevent name conflicts, Compose uses '[project_name]_[service_name]_[index]' formats.

quiz.question: Which section inside compose config declarations is used to define common networks?
quiz.explanation: The global 'networks:' section defines user-defined bridge or overlay topologies shared by services.

resources.title: Compose CLI Reference
resources.description: Comprehensive reference guide to all docker compose commands.

resources.title: Compose Specification Manual
resources.description: Manual detailing the YAML spec directives, schemas, and configurations.
